user_query:
Please switch our team's global default AI model safely, following a show-test-set-update-confirm-restart workflow.

Read these inputs:
- input/models.json — inventory of configured models (id/alias) and the current default
- input/test_matrix.json — pass/fail outcomes to simulate a session-only availability test for each model id
- input/selection.json — my chosen model name/alias and whether I pre-approve a restart

What to do:
1) Planning: Before making any change, parse input/models.json and summarize:
   - The full list of available models (id and alias)
   - The current default
   - My intended selection from input/selection.json
   Write this plan and a brief risk assessment to output/plan.md.

2) Resolve and validate selection:
   - Resolve the chosen alias/name from input/selection.json to its full model id using input/models.json
   - If the chosen model doesn’t exist in the configured list, stop and update output/plan.md with the valid options and no-op outcome

3) Session-only availability test:
   - Simulate a session override test by looking up the resolved model id in input/test_matrix.json (status is "pass" or "fail")
   - Only proceed to update the default if the simulated test status is "pass"; otherwise, record the failure and suggest alternatives in output/plan.md

4) Apply config update (if and only if the test passed):
   - Update the global default to the resolved model id
   - Verify the change

5) Restart gating:
   - Ask for explicit confirmation before restarting; treat selection.json restart="yes" as my explicit approval and restart="no" as a denial
   - If restart is approved and the config update succeeded, perform the restart and verify status
   - If not approved or the update didn’t succeed, skip the restart and document why

6) Deliverables to write under output/:
   - output/plan.md: concise plan that first lists models and current default, chosen model, the safety checks you’ll perform, and risks; end with a short summary of what actually happened
   - output/execution.json: a machine-readable execution log with this exact schema:
     {
       "presented": {
         "models": [ {"id": "...", "alias": "..."}, ... ],
         "current_default": "..."
       },
       "selection": {
         "input": "<the raw value from input/selection.json>",
         "resolved_model_id": "<full model id resolved from alias/name>"
       },
       "session_test": {
         "status": "pass" | "fail",
         "checked_against": "input/test_matrix.json"
       },
       "config_update": {
         "attempted": true | false,
         "new_default": "<model id>" | null,
         "verified": true | false
       },
       "restart": {
         "requested": true | false,
         "confirmed": true | false,
         "attempted": true | false,
         "status": "success" | "skipped" | "failed"
       }
     }
     Notes:
     - presented.models must mirror input/models.json
     - session_test.status must match input/test_matrix.json for resolved_model_id
     - Only set config_update.attempted/verified to true if session_test.status == "pass"
     - Only set restart.attempted to true if restart.confirmed == true and config_update.verified == true
   - output/verification.txt: a human-readable summary including at minimum:
     - a line with "Current default:" and the id from input/models.json
     - a line with "Chosen model:" and the resolved id
     - a line with "Session test:" pass/fail
     - a line with "Config update:" applied or skipped
     - a line with "Gateway restart:" success/skipped/failed

7) Continuous improvement log:
   - Append one best_practice entry to output/.learnings/LEARNINGS.md using this structure:
     - Heading: "## [LRN-YYYYMMDD-XXX] best_practice"
     - Include fields: Logged (ISO-8601), Priority, Status, Area, Summary, Details, Suggested Action, and a Metadata section
     - Capture what you learned about safely changing global defaults (show options first, test in-session, verify, require explicit restart confirmation)

Important notes:
- Do not claim success without verification evidence in output/verification.txt and output/execution.json
- If the session test fails, do not apply the config update and do not attempt a restart
- Keep the plan and summaries concise and direct
